new_capabilities: Added repeatable --function filters so exact branch-target scans can be constrained to specific function ranges or .pdata-resolved functions.
The scan payload now records function_filters, scan_range_count, and scan_ranges so focused branch artifacts are auditable.
Full-image branch scans remain the default, but split-state-machine work can now avoid whole-client decode costs.
tooling_milestone.why_added: A full-client branch scan for authCode split targets timed out, while the useful evidence lived in the narrow 0x14029E538..0x14029EA4C split family.

selector_source.meaning: The predecessor dispatch reads a selector/status value from controller child +0x50 +0x238.

branches.target_role: Split sub-entry that reads request/event +0x80/+0x88 and builds child +0x38 authCode/OTL continuation data.
Early dispatch/open path using request/event +0xD0 and the child +0x60 service route.
Neighbor split sub-entry that starts another request/event +0x80/+0x88 string-building pattern; not yet fully named.
State write path that sets [child+0x0] = 0x19 before jumping to the shared tail.

default_path.meaning: If no selector branch matches, the controller child state is set to 0x1A before the shared tail.

confirmed: 0x14029E894 is not an arbitrary mid-function block; it is a selector-owned split sub-entry reached from 0x14029E57D when child +0x50 +0x238 equals 0xC8.
0x14029E7C6 is reached from the same predecessor dispatcher when child +0x50 +0x238 equals 0x12E.
The predecessor dispatcher ties the authCode producer back to the controller child object through RDI = client +0x198D0 -> +0x110.
reversing_result.bounded_inference: The controller child has a status/response object at +0x50, and its +0x238 selector drives which login/web continuation body executes. The mapped selector cases include 0xC8 for the authCode source-span builder and 0x12E for the early URL/open dispatch path.
reversing_result.opennxt_relevance: This narrows the native 947 client's web-login continuation state machine: OpenNXT work can now separate the server-side response/status code that drives selector +0x238 from the later child +0x38 continuation string consumed by the client.

next_targets: Trace the 0x14029E5AC split body and its continuation in 0x14029E5D5..0x14029E7C6 to name selector case 0x1A6.
Trace writes to child +0x50 and status +0x238 to identify the server/web response object producer.
Use focused pe-branch-targets windows across neighboring split families to build a controller child selector/state table.
